name: Initialize a new AXIS project
prompt: Initialize a new AXIS project in the current working directory. Create an `axis.config.json` that points to a `./scenarios` directory and uses `claude-code` as the agent. Then create one scenario at `scenarios/url-fetch.json` that asks the agent to fetch https://example.com and describe what it sees. The scenario must include a `name`, a `prompt`, and a `rubric` with at least two items, each having a `check` field.

rubric.check: An `axis.config.json` file was created in the workspace root and contains valid JSON
The config includes a `scenarios` field pointing at `./scenarios` (or equivalent) and an `agents` array containing `claude-code`
A `scenarios/url-fetch.json` file was created and contains valid JSON
The scenario file has top-level `name`, `prompt`, and `rubric` fields
The rubric is an array with at least two items, each containing a `check` field

variants.name: with-docs
variants.prompt:
Initialize a new AXIS project in the current working directory. Create an `axis.config.json` that points to a `./scenarios` directory and uses `claude-code` as the agent. Then create one scenario at `scenarios/url-fetch.json` that asks the agent to fetch https://example.com and describe what it sees. The scenario must include a `name`, a `prompt`, and a `rubric` with at least two items, each having a `check` field.

Reference the AXIS documentation at https://axisproject.ai/quickstart for the project structure and configuration shape.
